content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, find a permutation P=P1,P2,…,PNP = P_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP=P1​,P2​,…,PN​ of length NNN that satisfies the following two conditions:


Pi≠iP_i \neq iPi​=i (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N);
Consider the set which contains PiP_iPi​ %\%% iii for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N. This set should have the **minimum** number of **distinct** integers possible, over all permutations PPP.
Note that a permutation of length NNN is an array containing every integer from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, an integer NNN, the length of the required permutation.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space-separated integers, denoting the permutation PPP of length NNN satisfying the above conditions.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T lines follow, each containing a single integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 10^5).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with N space‑separated integers – a permutation P of {1,…,N} such that Pi ≠ i for all i and the set {Pi % i | 1 ≤ i ≤ N} has the minimum possible number of distinct values.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=2 the cyclic shift gives [2,1]; remainders are {0,1}. For N=3 the cyclic shift gives [2,3,1]; remainders are {0,1}.
Cyclic shift for N=4 produces the required permutation with remainders {0,1}.

solution.approach: For any N ≥ 2 the cyclic shift permutation P = [2,3,4,…,N,1] satisfies all requirements. It is a derangement because no element stays in its original position. For i = 1 we have P1 = 2, thus 2 % 1 = 0. For every i ≥ 2, Pi = i+1 (or 1 when i = N) and (i+1) % i = 1, while 1 % N = 1 as well. Hence the set of remainders consists only of {0,1}, i.e. exactly two distinct values, which is the theoretical minimum (0 must appear for i = 1 and 0 cannot appear for i = 2 because Pi cannot be 2). The construction works in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(ΣN) ≤ 2·10^5
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the output array

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        perm = [str(i+1) for i in range(1, N)] + ['1']
        out_lines.append(' '.join(perm))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
